𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Fifteen — Part Three

The Book of Melakhim Bet

MELAKHIM BET

The Second Book of Kings

Chapters 14 through 19

The Fall of Samaria — Khizkiyahu — Sanheriv's Defeat — The Angel Strikes

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

No complete DSS manuscript for Melakhim Bet

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTERS 14 THROUGH 19 — THE FALL OF SAMARIA AND KHIZKIYAHU'S DELIVERANCE

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 14 Amazyahu of Yehudah — The Edomite Victory — Yo'ash Defeats Amazyahu — Yarovam II

1 In the second year of Yo'ash the son of Yeho'ahaz, king of Yisra'EL, Amazyahu the son of Yo'ash, king of Yehudah, began to reign.

2 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Yehoaddan of Yerushalayim.

3 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, yet not like Dawid his father. He did in all things as Yo'ash his father had done.

4 But the high places were not removed — the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.

5 And as soon as the royal power was firmly in his hand, he struck down his servants who had struck down the king his father.

6 But he did not put to death the children of the murderers, according to what is written in the Book of the Torah of Moshe, where YHWH commanded, Fathers shall not be put to death because of their children, nor shall children be put to death because of their fathers. But every person shall be put to death for his own sin.

7 He struck down ten thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt and took Sela by storm, and called it Yokte'el, which is its name to this day.

8 Then Amazyahu sent messengers to Yo'ash the son of Yeho'ahaz, son of Yehu, king of Yisra'EL, saying, Come, let us look one another in the face.

9 And Yo'ash the king of Yisra'EL sent word to Amazyahu king of Yehudah, A thistle on Lebanon sent to a cedar on Lebanon, saying, Give your daughter to my son for a wife. And a wild beast of Lebanon passed by and trampled down the thistle.

10 You have indeed struck down Edom, and your heart has lifted you up. Be content with your glory, and stay at home, for why should you provoke trouble so that you fall, you and Yehudah with you?

11 But Amazyahu would not listen. So Yo'ash king of Yisra'EL went up, and he and Amazyahu king of Yehudah faced one another in battle at Beit-Shemesh, which belongs to Yehudah.

12 And Yehudah was defeated by Yisra'EL, and every man fled to his home.

13 And Yo'ash king of Yisra'EL captured Amazyahu king of Yehudah, the son of Yo'ash, son of Akhazyahu, at Beit-Shemesh, and came to Yerushalayim and broke down the wall of Yerushalayim for four hundred cubits, from the Gate of Efrayim to the Corner Gate.

14 And he seized all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of YHWH and in the treasuries of the king's house, also hostages, and he returned to Samaria.

15 Now the rest of the acts of Yo'ash that he did, and his might, and how he fought with Amazyahu king of Yehudah, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

16 And Yo'ash slept with his fathers and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Yisra'EL, and Yarovam his son reigned in his place.

17 And Amazyahu the son of Yo'ash, king of Yehudah, lived fifteen years after the death of Yo'ash the son of Yeho'ahaz, king of Yisra'EL.

18 Now the rest of the deeds of Amazyahu, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

19 And they made a conspiracy against him in Yerushalayim, and he fled to Lakhish. But they sent after him to Lakhish and put him to death there.

20 And they brought him on horses, and he was buried in Yerushalayim with his fathers in the city of Dawid.

21 And all the people of Yehudah took Azaryahu, who was sixteen years old, and made him king instead of his father Amazyahu.

22 He built Elat and restored it to Yehudah, after the king slept with his fathers.

23 In the fifteenth year of Amazyahu the son of Yo'ash, king of Yehudah, Yarovam the son of Yo'ash, king of Yisra'EL, began to reign in Samaria, and he reigned forty-one years.

24 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH. He did not depart from all the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

25 He restored the border of Yisra'EL from Lebo-Khamath as far as the Sea of the Aravah, according to the word of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, which he spoke by his servant Yonah the son of Amittai, the prophet, who was from Gat-Khefer.

26 For YHWH saw that the affliction of Yisra'EL was very bitter, for there was none left, bond or free, and there was none to help Yisra'EL.

27 But YHWH had not said that he would blot out the name of Yisra'EL from under heaven, so he saved them by the hand of Yarovam the son of Yo'ash.

28 Now the rest of the acts of Yarovam and all that he did, and his might, how he fought, and how he restored Damascus and Khamath to Yehudah in Yisra'EL, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

29 And Yarovam slept with his fathers, the kings of Yisra'EL, and Zekhariah his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Verse 25 — the covenant restoration of Yisra'EL's borders through Yarovam II was according to the word of YHWH spoken by the prophet Yonah the son of Amittai from Gat-Khefer. This is the only reference to the prophet Yonah in the historical books, confirming that the book of Yonah preserves the ministry of a historical covenant prophet.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verse 6 — Amazyahu did not put to death the children of the murderers, according to what is written in the Book of the Torah of Moshe — is read as the covenant administration of Devarim 24:16. The king rules by the written Torah, not by the custom of political expediency.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 26-27 — YHWH saw the bitter affliction of Yisra'EL and had not said he would blot out their name — so he saved them by the hand of Yarovam. The covenant mercy of YHWH operates even through an evil king who did not depart from the sins of Yarovam. YHWH's faithfulness to the covenant name of Yisra'EL exceeds any individual king's character.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9-10 — Yo'ash's parable of the thistle and the cedar is a devastating piece of political wisdom: a thistle on Lebanon sent to a cedar, demanding a marriage alliance. A wild beast trampled the thistle. The parable says what diplomacy cannot: you have defeated a weaker opponent and mistaken it for greatness. Amazyahu's refusal to hear — verse 11 — and his subsequent defeat — verse 12 — is the covenant consequence of the pride that cannot receive wisdom. The gates of Yerushalayim are broken down — verse 13 — by Yisra'EL, not by an external enemy.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 15 Azaryahu of Yehudah Struck With Leprosy — The Final Kings of Yisra'EL — Tiglath-Pileser Invades

1 In the twenty-seventh year of Yarovam king of Yisra'EL, Azaryahu the son of Amazyahu, king of Yehudah, began to reign.

2 He was sixteen years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-two years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Yekholyahu of Yerushalayim.

3 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, according to all that his father Amazyahu had done.

4 Nevertheless, the high places were not taken away — the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.

5 And YHWH touched the king, so that he was a leper to the day of his death, and he lived in a separate house. And Yotam the king's son was over the household, governing the people of the land.

6 Now the rest of the acts of Azaryahu, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

7 And Azaryahu slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the city of Dawid, and Yotam his son reigned in his place.

8 In the thirty-eighth year of Azaryahu king of Yehudah, Zekhariah the son of Yarovam reigned over Yisra'EL in Samaria six months.

9 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, as his fathers had done. He did not depart from the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

10 Shallum the son of Yavesh conspired against him and struck him down before the people and killed him and reigned in his place.

11 Now the rest of the deeds of Zekhariah, behold, they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

12 This was the promise of YHWH that he gave to Yehu, Your sons of the fourth generation shall sit on the throne of Yisra'EL. And so it came to pass.

13 Shallum the son of Yavesh began to reign in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziyahu king of Yehudah, and he reigned one month in Samaria.

14 Then Menakhem the son of Gadi came up from Tirtzah and came to Samaria, and he struck down Shallum the son of Yavesh in Samaria and killed him and reigned in his place.

15 Now the rest of the deeds of Shallum, and the conspiracy that he made, behold, they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

16 At that time Menakhem destroyed Tifsakha and all who were in it and its territory from Tirtzah, because they did not open it to him. Therefore he destroyed it, and he ripped open all the pregnant women in it.

17 In the thirty-ninth year of Azaryahu king of Yehudah, Menakhem the son of Gadi began to reign over Yisra'EL, and he reigned ten years in Samaria.

18 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH. He did not depart all his days from all the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

19 Pul the king of Assyria came against the land, and Menakhem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver, that his hand might be with him to confirm his hold on the royal power.

20 Menakhem exacted the money from Yisra'EL, that is, from all the wealthy men, fifty shekels of silver from every man, to give to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria turned back and did not stay there in the land.

21 Now the rest of the deeds of Menakhem and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

22 And Menakhem slept with his fathers, and Pekakhyahu his son reigned in his place.

23 In the fiftieth year of Azaryahu king of Yehudah, Pekakhyahu the son of Menakhem began to reign over Yisra'EL in Samaria, and he reigned two years.

24 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH. He did not turn away from the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

25 And Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu, his captain, conspired against him with fifty men of the Gil'adites, and struck him down in Samaria, in the citadel of the king's house, also Argov and Arye — he killed him and reigned in his place.

26 Now the rest of the deeds of Pekakhyahu and all that he did, behold, they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

27 In the fifty-second year of Azaryahu king of Yehudah, Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu began to reign over Yisra'EL in Samaria, and he reigned twenty years.

28 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH. He did not depart from the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

29 In the days of Pekakh king of Yisra'EL, Tiglath-Pileser king of Assyria came and captured Iyon, Avel-Beit-Ma'akhah, Yanoakh, Kedesh, Khattzor, Gil'ad, and Galil, all the land of Naftali, and he carried the people captive to Assyria.

30 Then Hoshea the son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu and struck him down and killed him and reigned in his place, in the twentieth year of Yotam the son of Uzziyahu.

31 Now the rest of the deeds of Pekakh and all that he did, behold, they are written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

32 In the second year of Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu, king of Yisra'EL, Yotam the son of Uzziyahu, king of Yehudah, began to reign.

33 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Yerushah the daughter of Tzadok.

34 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, according to all that his father Uzziyahu had done.

35 Nevertheless, the high places were not removed — the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places. He built the upper gate of the house of YHWH.

36 Now the rest of the acts of Yotam and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

37 In those days YHWH began to send Retzin the king of Aram and Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu against Yehudah.

38 And Yotam slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of Dawid his father, and Akhaz his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 15 chronicles the accelerating political collapse of the northern kingdom — six kings in the final decades, four of them assassinated by their successors. The entry of Tiglath-Pileser III of Assyria in verse 29 is the beginning of the end for the northern kingdom, which will fall completely in Chapter 17.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Verse 12 — this was the promise of YHWH that he gave to Yehu, your sons of the fourth generation shall sit on the throne of Yisra'EL, and so it came to pass — is read as the covenant confirmation that YHWH's conditional blessings are fulfilled as precisely as his conditional judgments.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 19-20 — the first Assyrian tribute in the northern kingdom: Menakhem pays Tiglath-Pileser a thousand talents of silver, extracted from the wealthy at fifty shekels each. The covenant kingdom that began with YHWH's gift of the land now pays tribute to protect what remains of it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — YHWH touched the king, so that Azaryahu was a leper to the day of his death, and he lived in a separate house. The most prosperous and longest-reigning king of Yehudah in this period is struck with leprosy by YHWH. The Divrei HaYamim account explains: he entered the temple to burn incense — a priestly function — and was struck with leprosy when the priests confronted him. The covenant boundary between royal and priestly function, violated by the most successful Davidic king of the era, produces leprosy. Covenant success does not grant covenant exemption.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 16 Akhaz of Yehudah — He Burns His Son — Tiglath-Pileser Rescues Akhaz — The New Altar

1 In the seventeenth year of Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu, Akhaz the son of Yotam, king of Yehudah, began to reign.

2 Akhaz was twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim. And he did not do what was right in the eyes of YHWH his Creator, as his father Dawid had done,

3 But he walked in the way of the kings of Yisra'EL. He even burned his son as an offering, according to the despicable practices of the nations whom YHWH drove out before the people of Yisra'EL.

4 And he sacrificed and made offerings on the high places and on the hills and under every green tree.

5 Then Retzin the king of Aram and Pekakh the son of Remaliyahu, king of Yisra'EL, came up to wage war on Yerushalayim, and they besieged Akhaz but could not conquer him.

6 At that time Retzin the king of Aram recovered Elat for Aram and drove the men of Yehudah from Elat, and the Edomites came to Elat, where they dwell to this day.

7 So Akhaz sent messengers to Tiglath-Pileser king of Assyria, saying, I am your servant and your son. Come up and rescue me from the hand of the king of Aram and from the hand of the king of Yisra'EL, who are attacking me.

8 Akhaz also took the silver and gold that was found in the house of YHWH and in the treasures of the king's house and sent a present to the king of Assyria.

9 And the king of Assyria listened to him. The king of Assyria marched up against Damascus and took it, carrying its people captive to Kir, and he killed Retzin.

10 When King Akhaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-Pileser king of Assyria, he saw the altar that was at Damascus. And King Akhaz sent to Uriyah the priest a model of the altar, and its pattern, exact in all its details.

11 And Uriyah the priest built the altar — in accordance with all that King Akhaz had sent from Damascus, so Uriyah the priest made it, before King Akhaz arrived from Damascus.

12 And when the king came from Damascus, the king viewed the altar. Then the king drew near to the altar and went up on it

13 And burned his burnt offering and his grain offering and poured his drink offering and threw the blood of his peace offerings on the altar.

14 And the bronze altar that was before YHWH he removed from the front of the house, from the place between his altar and the house of YHWH, and put it on the north side of his new altar.

15 And King Akhaz commanded Uriyah the priest, saying, On the great altar burn the morning burnt offering and the evening grain offering and the king's burnt offering and his grain offering, with the burnt offering of all the people of the land, and their grain offering and their drink offering. And throw on it all the blood of the burnt offering and all the blood of the sacrifice, but the bronze altar shall be for me to inquire by.

16 Uriyah the priest did all this, as King Akhaz commanded.

17 And King Akhaz cut off the frames of the stands and removed the basin from them, and he took down the sea from off the bronze oxen that were under it and put it on a stone pedestal.

18 And the covered way for the Sabbath that had been built inside the house, and the outer entrance for the king, he removed from the house of YHWH, because of the king of Assyria.

19 Now the rest of the acts of Akhaz that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

20 And Akhaz slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of Dawid, and Khizkiyahu his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 16 documents the deepest covenant apostasy of the Davidic dynasty short of Shelomoh: Akhaz burns his son as an offering, appeals to Assyria rather than YHWH, and replaces the covenant altar in the temple with a copy of a Damascene altar. The covenant house built by Shelomoh is systematically paganized by Akhav's great-grandson.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verse 7 — Akhaz sent to Tiglath-Pileser, saying, I am your servant and your son — is read as the covenant renunciation. The Davidic king who should say I am YHWH's servant and son — Psalm 2:7 — instead says it to the Assyrian emperor. The covenant sonship is transferred from YHWH to Assyria.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 10-15 — Akhaz sees an altar in Damascus and has the Jerusalem altar replaced with a copy of it. The temple's covenant furnishings installed by Shelomoh and dedicated to YHWH's worship are either relocated, modified, or replaced. The priest Uriyah obeys the king rather than the Torah.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — I am your servant and your son. Come up and rescue me. Akhaz uses the covenant language of the Davidic relationship — son — to describe his submission to Assyria. The covenant that YHWH declared to Dawid in 2 Shemu'el 7:14 — I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son — is now applied by a Davidic king to a foreign emperor. Akhaz also empties the temple treasury to pay his new master — verse 8. The prophecy of Yesha'yahu during Akhaz's reign — the full account in Yesha'yahu 7 — documents the covenant alternative Akhaz refused: ask a sign from YHWH. He refused and went to Assyria instead.

THE FALL OF SAMARIA AND KHIZKIYAHU — CHAPTERS 17 THROUGH 19

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 17 The Fall of Samaria — Yisra'EL Carried Away to Assyria — The Covenant Diagnosis — Foreign Nations Settle

1 In the twelfth year of Akhaz king of Yehudah, Hoshea the son of Elah began to reign in Samaria over Yisra'EL, and he reigned nine years.

2 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, yet not as the kings of Yisra'EL who were before him.

3 Against him came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria. And Hoshea became his vassal and paid him tribute.

4 But the king of Assyria found treachery in Hoshea, for he had sent messengers to So, king of Egypt, and offered no tribute to the king of Assyria, as he had done year by year. Therefore the king of Assyria shut him up and bound him in prison.

5 Then the king of Assyria invaded all the land and came to Samaria, and for three years he besieged it.

6 In the ninth year of Hoshea, the king of Assyria captured Samaria, and he carried Yisra'EL away to Assyria and placed them in Khala, and on the Khavor, the river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.

7 And this occurred because the people of Yisra'EL had sinned against YHWH their Creator, who had brought them up out of the land of Egypt from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and had feared other gods

8 And walked in the customs of the nations whom YHWH drove out before the people of Yisra'EL, and in the customs that the kings of Yisra'EL had practiced.

9 And the people of Yisra'EL did secretly against YHWH their Creator things that were not right. They built for themselves high places in all their towns, from watchtower to fortified city.

10 They set up for themselves pillars and Asherim on every high hill and under every green tree,

11 And there they made offerings on all the high places, as the nations did whom YHWH carried away before them. And they did wicked things, provoking YHWH to anger,

12 And they served idols, of which YHWH had said to them, You shall not do this.

13 Yet YHWH warned Yisra'EL and Yehudah by every prophet and every seer, saying, Turn from your evil ways and keep my commandments and my statutes, in accordance with all the Torah that I commanded your fathers, and that I sent to you by my servants the prophets.

14 But they would not listen, but were stubborn, as their fathers had been, who did not believe in YHWH their Creator.

15 They despised his statutes and his covenant that he made with their fathers and the warnings that he gave them. They went after false idols and became false, and they followed the nations that were around them, concerning whom YHWH had commanded them that they should not do like them.

16 And they abandoned all the commandments of YHWH their Creator, and made for themselves metal images of two calves — and they made an Asherah and worshiped all the host of heaven and served Ba'al.

17 And they burned their sons and their daughters as offerings and used divination and omens and sold themselves to do evil in the sight of YHWH, provoking him to anger.

18 Therefore YHWH was very angry with Yisra'EL and removed them out of his sight. None was left but the tribe of Yehudah only.

19 Yehudah also did not keep the commandments of YHWH their Creator, but walked in the customs that Yisra'EL had introduced.

20 And YHWH rejected all the descendants of Yisra'EL and afflicted them and gave them into the hand of plunderers, until he had cast them out of his sight.

21 When he had torn Yisra'EL from the house of Dawid, they made Yarovam the son of Nevat king. And Yarovam drove Yisra'EL from following YHWH and made them commit great sin.

22 The people of Yisra'EL walked in all the sins that Yarovam did. They did not depart from them,

23 Until YHWH removed Yisra'EL out of his sight, as he had spoken by all his servants the prophets. So Yisra'EL was exiled from their own land to Assyria until this day.

24 And the king of Assyria brought people from Babylon, Kutah, Avva, Khamath, and Sefarvaim, and placed them in the cities of Samaria instead of the people of Yisra'EL. And they took possession of Samaria and lived in its cities.

25 And at the beginning of their dwelling there, they did not fear YHWH. Therefore YHWH sent lions among them, which killed some of them.

26 So the king of Assyria was told, The nations that you have carried away and placed in the cities of Samaria do not know the law of the god of the land. Therefore he has sent lions among them, and behold, they are killing them, because they do not know the law of the god of the land.

27 Then the king of Assyria commanded, Send there one of the priests whom you carried away from there, and let him go and dwell there and teach them the law of the god of the land.

28 So one of the priests whom they had carried away from Samaria came and lived in Beit-El and taught them how they should fear YHWH.

29 But every nation still made gods of its own and put them in the shrines of the high places that the Samaritans had made, every nation in the cities in which they lived.

30 The men of Babylon made Sukkot-Benot, the men of Kut made Nergal, the men of Khamath made Ashima,

31 The Avvites made Nivkhaz and Tartaq — and the Sefarvites burned their children in the fire to Adrammelekh and Anammelekh, the gods of Sefarvaim.

32 They also feared YHWH and appointed from among themselves all sorts of people as priests of the high places, who sacrificed for them in the shrines of the high places.

33 So they feared YHWH but also served their own gods, after the manner of the nations from among whom they had been carried away.

34 To this day they do according to the former manner. They do not fear YHWH, and they do not follow the statutes or the rules or the Torah or the commandment that YHWH commanded the children of Yaakov, whom he named Yisra'EL.

35 YHWH made a covenant with them and commanded them, You shall not fear other gods or bow yourselves to them or serve them or sacrifice to them,

36 But you shall fear YHWH, who brought you out of the land of Egypt with great power and with an outstretched arm. You shall bow yourselves to him, and to him you shall sacrifice.

37 And the statutes and the rules and the Torah and the commandment that he wrote for you, you shall always be careful to do. You shall not fear other gods,

38 And you shall not forget the covenant that I have made with you. You shall not fear other gods,

39 But you shall fear YHWH your Creator, and he will deliver you out of the hand of all your enemies.

40 However, they would not listen, but they did according to their former manner.

41 So these nations feared YHWH and also served their carved images. Their children did likewise, and their children's children — as their fathers did, so they do to this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 17 is the most extensive covenant theological analysis in the entire Nevi'im — a sustained explanation of why YHWH removed Yisra'EL from the land. Verses 7-23 constitute the covenant diagnosis: a catalog of every form of covenant violation committed over two centuries, from the high places to the burning of children.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. Verse 13 — YHWH warned Yisra'EL and Yehudah by every prophet and every seer, saying, turn from your evil ways — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's persistent mercy before judgment. The exile was not a sudden decision — it was the final answer to two centuries of covenant warning systematically ignored.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 33 — they feared YHWH but also served their own gods, after the manner of the nations. The covenant syncretism that Melakhim has documented throughout the northern kingdom's history is now institutionalized among the foreign settlers. Half-devotion to YHWH alongside devotion to other gods is the covenant definition of failure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13-14 — yet YHWH warned Yisra'EL and Yehudah by every prophet and every seer — but they would not listen, but were stubborn. The exile is not arbitrary. It is the end point of a covenant process: election, covenant, warning, persistent violation, patient warning through prophets, continued violation, and finally judgment. YHWH sent every prophet — this is the covenant declaration that the exile was not a covenant surprise. It was the covenant consequence of the covenant condition stated from the beginning. Verse 23 — until YHWH removed Yisra'EL out of his sight, as he had spoken by all his servants the prophets. Every prophetic word about the exile — from Akhiyah to Eliyahu to Elisha to Amos to Hosea — was the word of YHWH reaching its fulfillment.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 18 Khizkiyahu — He Trusted in YHWH — Sanheriv Invades — The Rabshakeh's Taunts — Do Not Listen to Khizkiyahu

1 In the third year of Hoshea son of Elah, king of Yisra'EL, Khizkiyahu the son of Akhaz, king of Yehudah, began to reign.

2 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Avi the daughter of Zekhariah.

3 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, according to all that Dawid his father had done.

4 He removed the high places and broke the pillars and cut down the Asherah. And he broke in pieces the bronze serpent that Moshe had made, for until those days the people of Yisra'EL had made offerings to it — it was called Nakhushtan.

5 He trusted in YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, so that there was none like him among all the kings of Yehudah after him, nor among those who were before him.

6 For he held fast to YHWH. He did not depart from following him, but kept the commandments that YHWH commanded Moshe.

7 And YHWH was with him — wherever he went out, he prospered. He rebelled against the king of Assyria and would not serve him.

8 He struck down the Pelishtim as far as Azzah and its territory, from watchtower to fortified city.

9 In the fourth year of King Khizkiyahu, which was the seventh year of Hoshea son of Elah, king of Yisra'EL, Shalmaneser king of Assyria came up against Samaria and besieged it,

10 And at the end of three years he took it. In the sixth year of Khizkiyahu, which was the ninth year of Hoshea king of Yisra'EL, Samaria was taken.

11 The king of Assyria carried Yisra'EL away to Assyria and put them in Khala, and on the Khavor, the river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes,

12 Because they did not obey the voice of YHWH their Creator but transgressed his covenant, even all that Moshe the servant of YHWH commanded. They neither listened nor obeyed.

13 In the fourteenth year of King Khizkiyahu, Sanheriv king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Yehudah and took them.

14 And Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah sent to the king of Assyria at Lakhish, saying, I have done wrong — withdraw from me. Whatever you impose on me I will bear. And the king of Assyria required of Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah three hundred talents of silver and thirty talents of gold.

15 And Khizkiyahu gave him all the silver that was found in the house of YHWH and in the treasuries of the king's house.

16 At that time Khizkiyahu stripped the gold from the doors of the temple of YHWH and from the doorposts that Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah had overlaid and gave it to the king of Assyria.

17 And the king of Assyria sent the Tartan, the Rav-Saris, and the Rabshakeh with a great army from Lakhish to King Khizkiyahu at Yerushalayim. And they went up and came to Yerushalayim. When they arrived, they came and stood by the conduit of the upper pool, which is on the highway to the Washer's Field.

18 And when they called for the king, there came out to them Elyakim the son of Khilkiyahu, who was over the household, and Shevna the secretary, and Yo'akh the son of Asaf, the recorder.

19 And the Rabshakeh said to them, Say to Khizkiyahu, Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria: On what do you rest this trust of yours?

20 Do you think that mere words are strategy and power for war? In whom do you now trust, that you have rebelled against me?

21 Behold, you are trusting now in Egypt, that broken reed of a staff, which will pierce the hand of any man who leans on it. Such is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who trust in him.

22 But if you say to me, We trust in YHWH our Creator, is it not he whose high places and altars Khizkiyahu has removed, telling Yehudah and Yerushalayim, You shall worship before this altar in Yerushalayim?

23 Come now, make a wager with my master the king of Assyria: I will give you two thousand horses, if you are able on your part to set riders on them.

24 How then can you repulse a single captain among the least of my master's servants, when you trust in Egypt for chariots and for horsemen?

25 Moreover, is it without YHWH that I have come up against this place to destroy it? YHWH said to me, Go up against this land, and destroy it.

26 Then Elyakim the son of Khilkiyahu, and Shevna, and Yo'akh said to the Rabshakeh, Please speak to your servants in Aramaic, for we understand it. Do not speak to us in the language of Yehudah within the hearing of the people who are on the wall.

27 But the Rabshakeh said to them, Has my master sent me to speak these words to your master and to you, and not to the men sitting on the wall, who are doomed with you to eat their own dung and drink their own urine?

28 Then the Rabshakeh stood and called out in a loud voice in the language of Yehudah: Hear the word of the great king, the king of Assyria.

29 Thus says the king, Do not let Khizkiyahu deceive you, for he will not be able to deliver you out of his hand.

30 Do not let Khizkiyahu make you trust in YHWH by saying, YHWH will surely deliver us, and this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.

31 Do not listen to Khizkiyahu, for thus says the king of Assyria: Make your peace with me and come out to me. Then each one of you will eat of his own vine, and each one of his own fig tree, and each one of you will drink the water of his own cistern,

32 Until I come and take you away to a land like your own land, a land of grain and wine, a land of bread and vineyards, a land of olive trees and honey, that you may live, and not die. And do not listen to Khizkiyahu when he misleads you by saying, YHWH will deliver us.

33 Has any of the gods of the nations ever delivered his land out of the hand of the king of Assyria?

34 Where are the gods of Khamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sefarvaim, Hena, and Ivvah? Have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?

35 Who among all the gods of the lands have delivered their lands out of my hand, that YHWH should deliver Yerushalayim out of my hand?

36 But the people were silent and answered him not a word, for the king's command was, Do not answer him.

37 Then Elyakim the son of Khilkiyahu, who was over the household, and Shevna the secretary, and Yo'akh the son of Asaf, the recorder, came to Khizkiyahu with their clothes torn and told him the words of the Rabshakeh.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 18 introduces Khizkiyahu — the greatest covenant king of Yehudah after Dawid — with the most comprehensive positive evaluation in the entire Nevi'im: there was none like him among all the kings of Yehudah after him nor among those before him — verse 5. The Rabshakeh's taunt and Khizkiyahu's covenant response form the theological confrontation at the center of the entire Khizkiyahu narrative.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 4 — Khizkiyahu broke in pieces the bronze serpent that Moshe had made, for until those days the people had made offerings to it — is read as the covenant reformation that required destroying what YHWH had once commanded. The bronze serpent of Bamidbar 21:8-9 had become an idol. Covenant faithfulness required its destruction.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 25 — the Rabshakeh's claim that YHWH sent him — is the most theologically sophisticated element of the Assyrian taunt: he acknowledges YHWH but claims covenant authorization for the invasion. This is either political propaganda or the covenant reality that YHWH uses Assyria as the rod of his anger — Yesha'yahu 10:5.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 36 — the people were silent and answered him not a word, for the king's command was, do not answer him. Khizkiyahu's command — do not engage the Rabshakeh's theological provocations — is the covenant wisdom of silence before blasphemy. The taunt is designed to produce either capitulation or futile argument. Khizkiyahu refuses both. He will take the matter directly to YHWH in Chapter 19. Verse 31 — the Rabshakeh offers a covenant-sounding alternative: every man under his vine and fig tree — the very image of Shelomoh's covenant peace in Melakhim Aleph 4:25. Assyria weaponizes the covenant vocabulary of peace to seduce surrender.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 19 Khizkiyahu's Prayer — Yesha'yahu's Word — Sanheriv's Letter — The Angel Strikes — 185,000 Slain

1 As soon as King Khizkiyahu heard it, he tore his clothes and covered himself with sackcloth and went into the house of YHWH.

2 And he sent Elyakim, who was over the household, and Shevna the secretary, and the senior priests, covered with sackcloth, to the prophet Yesha'yahu the son of Amoz.

3 They said to him, Thus says Khizkiyahu, This day is a day of distress, of rebuke, and of disgrace — children have come to the point of birth, and there is no strength to bring them forth.

4 It may be that YHWH your Creator will hear all the words of the Rabshakeh, whom his master the king of Assyria has sent to mock the living Creator, and will rebuke the words that YHWH your Creator has heard. Therefore lift up your prayer for the remnant that is left.

5 When the servants of King Khizkiyahu came to Yesha'yahu,

6 Yesha'yahu said to them, Say to your master, Thus says YHWH: Do not be afraid because of the words that you have heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have reviled me.

7 Behold, I will put a spirit in him, so that he shall hear a rumor and return to his own land, and I will make him fall by the sword in his own land.

8 The Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria fighting against Livnah, for he had heard that the king had left Lakhish.

9 And when the king heard concerning Tirhakah king of Kush, Behold, he has set out to fight against you, he sent messengers again to Khizkiyahu, saying,

10 Thus shall you speak to Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah: Do not let your Creator in whom you trust deceive you by saying, Yerushalayim will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.

11 Behold, you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, devoting them to destruction. And shall you be delivered?

12 Have the gods of the nations delivered them, the nations that my fathers destroyed, Gozan, Kharan, Retzef, and the people of Eden who were in Telassar?

13 Where is the king of Khamath, the king of Arpad, the king of the city of Sefarvaim, the king of Hena, or the king of Ivvah?

14 Khizkiyahu received the letter from the hand of the messengers and read it. And Khizkiyahu went up to the house of YHWH and spread it before YHWH.

15 And Khizkiyahu prayed before YHWH and said, O YHWH, Creator of Yisra'EL, enthroned above the cherubim, you are the Creator, you alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth — you have made heaven and earth.

16 Incline your ear, O YHWH, and hear — open your eyes, O YHWH, and see — and hear the words of Sanheriv, which he has sent to mock the living Creator.

17 Truly, O YHWH, the kings of Assyria have laid waste the nations and their lands

18 And have cast their gods into the fire, for they were not gods, but the work of men's hands, wood and stone. Therefore they were destroyed.

19 So now, O YHWH our Creator, save us, please, from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you, O YHWH, are Creator alone.

20 Then Yesha'yahu the son of Amoz sent to Khizkiyahu, saying, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Your prayer to me about Sanheriv king of Assyria I have heard.

21 This is the word that YHWH has spoken concerning him: She despises you, she scorns you — the virgin daughter of Tziyon — she wags her head behind you, the daughter of Yerushalayim.

22 Whom have you mocked and reviled? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes to the heights? Against the Holy One of Yisra'EL.

23 By your messengers you have mocked the Lord, and you have said, With my many chariots I have gone up the heights of the mountains, to the far recesses of Lebanon, to cut down its tallest cedars, its choicest cypresses — I entered its farthest lodging place, its most fruitful forest.

24 I dug wells and drank the waters, and I dried up with the sole of my foot all the streams of Egypt.

25 Have you not heard that I determined it long ago? I planned from days of old what now I bring to pass, that you should make fortified cities crash into heaps of ruins,

26 While their inhabitants, shorn of strength, are dismayed and confounded, and have become like plants of the field and like tender grass, like grass on the housetops, blighted before it is grown.

27 But I know your sitting down and your going out and coming in, and your raging against me.

28 Because you have raged against me and your complacency has come to my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth, and I will turn you back on the way by which you came.

29 And this shall be the sign for you: this year you shall eat what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs of the same. Then in the third year sow and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.

30 And the surviving remnant of the house of Yehudah shall again take root downward and bear fruit upward.

31 For out of Yerushalayim shall go a remnant, and out of Mount Tziyon a band of survivors. The zeal of YHWH of hosts will do this.

32 Therefore thus says YHWH concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city or shoot an arrow there, or come before it with a shield or cast up a siege mound against it.

33 By the way that he came, by the same he shall return, and he shall not come into this city, declares YHWH.

34 For I will defend this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of my servant Dawid.

35 And that night the angel of YHWH went out and struck down a hundred and eighty-five thousand in the camp of the Assyrians. And when people arose early in the morning, behold, these were all dead bodies.

36 Then Sanheriv king of Assyria departed and went home and lived at Nineveh.

37 And as he was worshiping in the house of Nisrokh his god, Adrammelekh and Sharetzer, his sons, struck him down with the sword and escaped into the land of Ararat. And Esarhaddon his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 19 contains the most dramatic covenant reversal in the Nevi'im: the Assyrian empire that had devoured nation after nation is stopped before Yerushalayim by a single angel in a single night. Verse 35 — the angel of YHWH struck down 185,000 — is the largest single-event covenant deliverance since the parting of the Red Sea.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 14-19 — Khizkiyahu's prayer — spreading the Assyrian letter before YHWH and praying — is read as the covenant model of crisis prayer: bring the physical threat into YHWH's presence, acknowledge his sovereignty over all the earth, and ask for deliverance so that all the kingdoms may know that YHWH alone is Creator.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 34 — for I will defend this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of my servant Dawid. YHWH's defense of Yerushalayim is grounded in two covenant realities: his own name and the covenant with Dawid. The city is saved not by Khizkiyahu's righteousness alone but by the covenant purposes that transcend any single king.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — Khizkiyahu received the Assyrian letter and spread it before YHWH. This single act is the covenant act that determines the outcome. The letter is a declaration of power — you have heard what Assyria has done to all lands. Khizkiyahu's response is to take it literally to YHWH's presence and lay it before him. The political threat becomes a covenant prayer opportunity. Every claim Sanheriv makes against YHWH's power becomes the content of the prayer that brings YHWH's power into action. Verse 25 — have you not heard that I determined it long ago? YHWH's response claims the Assyrian conquests themselves as YHWH's predetermined acts. Sanheriv's victories were not his own power — they were YHWH's instrument. The same instrument is now stopped at YHWH's chosen city.

END OF PART THREE — CHAPTERS 14 THROUGH 19
